Clan * clan_vnum_lookup(const Vnum &vnum.brief: Looks up and returns the Clan associated with a given virtual number (vnum).
Clan * clan_vnum_lookup(const Vnum &vnum.details: This function searches through the linked list of Clan objects to find a clan whose area vnum range includes the specified vnum. It iterates from the head to the tail of the clan list, comparing the input vnum against each clan's minimum and maximum vnum bounds. If a matching clan is found, it is returned; otherwise, the function returns nullptr, indicating no clan covers the provided vnum.

params.vnum: A constant reference to a Vnum object representing the virtual number to look up.

Clan * clan_vnum_lookup(const Vnum &vnum.returns: A pointer to the Clan object whose vnum range includes the specified vnum, or nullptr if no such clan exists.
Clan * clan_vnum_lookup(const Vnum &vnum.name: clan_vnum_lookup

Clan * clan_lookup(const String &name).brief: Looks up and returns a pointer to a Clan object whose name matches the given prefix.
Clan * clan_lookup(const String &name).details: This function searches through the global clan table for a Clan whose name begins with the specified prefix. It iterates from the head to the tail of the clan list, utilizing the String::is_prefix_of method to determine prefix matches. If a matching Clan is found, a pointer to that Clan is returned; otherwise, nullptr is returned, indicating no match was found. The function handles empty input by immediately returning nullptr.

params.name: The String object representing the prefix of the clan name to search for.

Clan * clan_lookup(const String &name).returns: A pointer to the Clan object whose name matches the prefix, or nullptr if no such Clan exists.
Clan * clan_lookup(const String &name).name: clan_lookup

int position_lookup(const String &name).brief: Finds the position index of a given name within the position table.
int position_lookup(const String &name).details: This function searches through the global position_table to locate the index of an entry whose name matches the provided name argument. It performs a case-insensitive comparison of the first character and checks if the provided name is a prefix of the table entry's name. If a matching entry is found, the function returns its index; otherwise, it returns -1 indicating no match was found.

params.name: The String object representing the name to look up in the position table.

int position_lookup(const String &name).returns: The index of the matching entry in position_table if found; otherwise, -1 indicating no match.
int position_lookup(const String &name).name: position_lookup

int sex_lookup(const String &name).brief: Returns the index of the sex entry matching the given name, or -1 if not found.
int sex_lookup(const String &name).details: This function searches through the global 'sex_table' to find an entry whose name has a prefix matching the provided 'name' parameter, ignoring case differences in the first character. It returns the index of the matching entry if found; otherwise, it returns -1. The search is case-insensitive and considers only the initial character and prefix match for identification.

params.name: A String object representing the name to look up in the sex table.

int sex_lookup(const String &name).returns: The index of the matching sex entry in 'sex_table' if found; otherwise, -1 indicating no match.
int sex_lookup(const String &name).name: sex_lookup

int size_lookup(const String &name).brief: Returns the index of the size table entry matching the given name prefix.
int size_lookup(const String &name).details: This function searches through the global size_table to find an entry whose name shares a prefix with the provided String 'name', ignoring case differences. It compares the first character case-insensitively and checks if 'name' is a prefix of the table entry's name. If a match is found, it returns the index of that entry; otherwise, it returns -1 indicating no match was found.

params.name: A String object representing the name to be matched against entries in the size_table.

int size_lookup(const String &name).returns: The index of the matching size_table entry if found; otherwise, -1 indicating no match.
int size_lookup(const String &name).name: size_lookup

String condition_lookup(int condition).brief: Returns a descriptive string based on the input condition value.
String condition_lookup(int condition).details: This function evaluates the integer 'condition' parameter and returns a corresponding String object that describes the condition's qualitative state. The returned string indicates the condition level, such as 'perfect', 'good', 'average', etc., based on predefined threshold ranges. It serves as a lookup utility to translate numerical condition scores into human-readable descriptions.

params.condition: An integer representing the condition score to be evaluated.

String condition_lookup(int condition).returns: A String object containing the description of the condition corresponding to the input value.
String condition_lookup(int condition).name: condition_lookup

String sector_lookup(Sector type).brief: Retrieves the name of a sector based on its type enumeration.
String sector_lookup(Sector type).details: This function searches through the global 'sector_table' to find an entry with a matching 'Sector' type. If a matching entry is found, it returns the associated name as a String object. If no match is found, it returns the string 'unknown'. The function provides a way to map sector types to their human-readable names for use in various parts of the application.

params.type: The Sector enumeration value representing the specific sector type to look up.

String sector_lookup(Sector type).returns: A String containing the name of the sector corresponding to the provided type, or 'unknown' if no matching sector is found.
String sector_lookup(Sector type).name: sector_lookup

int race_lookup(const String &name).brief: Returns the index of the race in the race table that matches the given name prefix.
int race_lookup(const String &name).details: This function searches through the global race table to find a race whose name starts with the provided name string, ignoring case differences. It compares the first character of the input name with each race name's first character, then checks if the input name is a prefix of the race name. If a match is found, it returns the index of that race; otherwise, it returns 0, indicating no match was found.

params.name: A String object representing the race name to look up.

int race_lookup(const String &name).returns: An integer representing the index of the matching race in the race table; returns 0 if no match is found.
int race_lookup(const String &name).name: race_lookup

int weapon_lookup(const String &name).brief: Finds the index of a weapon in the weapon table based on its name.
int weapon_lookup(const String &name).details: This function searches through the global weapon_table to locate a weapon whose name matches the provided name parameter. It performs a case-insensitive comparison of the first character and checks if the provided name is a prefix of the weapon's name. If a matching weapon is found, the function returns its index within the table; otherwise, it returns -1 to indicate no match was found.

params.name: The name of the weapon to look up, provided as a String object.

int weapon_lookup(const String &name).returns: The index of the matching weapon in the weapon_table if found; otherwise, -1 indicating no match.
int weapon_lookup(const String &name).name: weapon_lookup

int item_lookup(const String &name).brief: Looks up an item by name in the item table and returns its type identifier.
int item_lookup(const String &name).details: This function searches through the global item_table to find an item whose name matches the provided name argument. It performs a case-insensitive comparison, checking if the first character matches and if the name is a prefix of the item's name. If a matching item is found, the function returns the item's type identifier; otherwise, it returns -1 to indicate that the item was not found.

params.name: The name of the item to look up, provided as a String object.

int item_lookup(const String &name).returns: The integer type identifier of the matched item if found; otherwise, -1 indicating no match.
int item_lookup(const String &name).name: item_lookup

int attack_lookup(const String &name).brief: Returns the index of the attack in the attack table that matches the given name prefix.
int attack_lookup(const String &name).details: This function searches through the global attack_table to find an attack whose name has a prefix matching the provided name, ignoring case differences. It compares the first character of the input name with each attack's name, and if they match (case-insensitive), it further checks if the input name is a prefix of the attack's name. If a match is found, it returns the index of that attack; otherwise, it returns 0, indicating no match was found.

params.name: A String object representing the attack name to look up.

int attack_lookup(const String &name).returns: The index of the matching attack in attack_table if found; otherwise, 0.
int attack_lookup(const String &name).name: attack_lookup

Guild guild_lookup(const String &name).brief: Determines the guild corresponding to a given name prefix.
Guild guild_lookup(const String &name).details: This function searches through the list of available guilds to find one whose name is a prefix of the provided String 'name'. It iterates over all guilds defined in the 'Guild' enumeration, comparing each guild's name with the input. If a matching prefix is found, it returns the corresponding 'Guild' enumeration value. If no match is found, it returns 'Guild::none'. This function is useful for mapping user-inputted or partial guild names to their internal enumeration representation.

params.name: The String object containing the name or prefix to match against guild names.

Guild guild_lookup(const String &name).returns: The 'Guild' enumeration value that corresponds to the matched guild name prefix, or 'Guild::none' if no match is found.
Guild guild_lookup(const String &name).name: guild_lookup

int liq_lookup(const String &name).brief: Looks up the index of a specified name in the liquid table.
int liq_lookup(const String &name).details: This function searches through the global liq_table to find an entry whose name matches the provided String 'name'. It compares the first character case-insensitively and checks if 'name' is a prefix of the table entry's name, considering at least one character. If a matching entry is found, the function returns its index; otherwise, it returns -1 indicating no match was found.

params.name: The String object representing the name to look up in the liquid table.

int liq_lookup(const String &name).returns: The index of the matching liquid in liq_table if found; otherwise, -1 indicating no match.
int liq_lookup(const String &name).name: liq_lookup

int deity_lookup(const String &name).brief: Finds the index of the deity whose name has the given prefix.
int deity_lookup(const String &name).details: This function searches through the global deity_table to identify a deity whose name starts with the specified prefix. It returns the index of the first matching deity. If no deity's name matches the prefix, the function returns -1. The comparison is case-insensitive and relies on the String::is_prefix_of method to determine prefix matches.

params.name: The String object representing the prefix of the deity's name to search for.

int deity_lookup(const String &name).returns: The index of the first deity in deity_table whose name has the given prefix; returns -1 if no match is found.
int deity_lookup(const String &name).name: deity_lookup
